title: Kaworus Character split from Did Not Like
display_title: Kaworus Character split from Did Not Like
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu's Character [split from Did Not Like]             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri Feb 05, 2010 1:33 pm [Split from here .] Halicat wrote: Erm I don't really know what I don't like about the show, but I guess I would have liked it more if we got to see more of Kaworu, I felt they portrayed him as a very interesting character with a lot going on and he was a nice breath of cheery air when everything was going to shit. Seeing his relationship with Shinji grow a little more before his eventual betrayal and death would have been much more dramatic for me then.

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Sun Feb 07, 2010 9:41 pm I agree. Also, to something said way earlier, I didn't think it was obvious that Kaworu was an angel when I first saw the show because I was more concerned with the fact that he looked like Rei and that Rei was a clone thus Kaworu. And I don't think anyone at the point thought that Rei was actually Lilith. For me, the realization flowed that Kaworu was like Rei -> Kaworu was an angel -> Rei was an angel. Looking back, there were so many clues (like him sitting on the angel statue) but then I wasn't considering that as a possibility at all because I fail to watch things with an analytical eye most of the time. Especially if I am "loving" what I am watching.

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Mon Feb 08, 2010 1:42 am I had no problems with the way Kaworu was placed in the series. I do, however, have a problem with the incredible length of time between the end of the Shinji/Kaworu dialogue and Kaworu's crushing. I understand the dramatic effect, but did it really need to be that long?

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Mon Feb 08, 2010 8:48 am child of Lilith wrote: I don’t know if expanding Kaworu’s role would really be a good thing. It would be nice to see more of him and Shinji together but I think having him in the show for such a short time was the whole point of him being there. Having him in the show longer could risk damaging his character and marginalizing the effect his death created. I don’t think the viewer is supposed to get a good grasp on who he is. His mysteries nature is what makes his death all the more interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Mon Feb 08, 2010 10:02 am Manga Kaworu is awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Mon Feb 08, 2010 12:05 pm I need to read the manga, I'd probably like him too since I figure the manga just retells the same characters with different aspects to their personality :)

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Mon Feb 08, 2010 2:30 pm The character dynamic is rather different too; for instance, Shinji and Kaworu fight like a tsundere couple.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Feb 08, 2010 9:15 pm I hate manga Kaworu. They turned him into a complete ass. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Tue Feb 09, 2010 1:41 am Lucretius wrote: The character dynamic is rather different too; for instance, Shinji and Kaworu fight like a tsundere couple.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 09, 2010 2:32 am I’m afraid I’m going to have to disagree with you on that. I think Sadamaoto completely failed in his attempted to make him more human. He tried to give him flaws in order to make him more relatable to the rest of the cast, but all he succeed in doing was giving him clay feet. His new flaws just made him unlikable and rather pathetic in my view. Less a well-rounded character and more of a deliberate insult to the original. Lets not forget Sadamaoto admitted he didn’t like anime Kawaru in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Tue Feb 09, 2010 3:48 am I'm not sure if manga Kaworu is supposed to be more human; he's less ethereal, sure, but he's also more alien. Kaworu doesn't see why Shinji would be upset about Rei and Asuka's fates, which suggests a pretty non-human mindset. I actually think Sadamoto did a much better job than the TV series of portraying a space god in human form; his morals aren't like ours.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Feb 09, 2010 6:04 am @Child of Lilith: Could you please elaborate a bit on the Sadamoto admitted he didn't like Anime Kaworu part? Lucretius wrote: The character dynamic is rather different too; for instance, Shinji and Kaworu fight like a tsundere couple.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Feb 09, 2010 12:23 pm I actually think Sadamoto did a much better job than the TV series of portraying a space god in human form; his morals aren't like ours.

[]:             Quote Post by Lament » Tue Feb 09, 2010 1:53 pm LiLi wrote: I would have liked to see more of AnimeKaworu. For one thing, I find him fascinating and amusing at the same time. I think giving him more screentime at least in the one episode dedicated to him, might have helped in lessening the "Kaworu is not a character, he's just a plot device" effect his appearance had on some viewers.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 09, 2010 1:55 pm Lucretius: I think Sadamoto was angling to make him more human but failed. He made him different, but in a stupid way. His ignorance of how humans think wasn’t portrayed in a practically flattering way in my opinion. His ignorance just set him up as an object of ridicule and score in my eyes. LiLi: I don’t remember the exact palace I read it, but Sadamoto said something to the effect he didn’t like or trust him. I think it would be foolish to believe this didn’t have a negative impact on how he portrayed him in his manga. AuraTwilight: I think you hit the nail on the head with your post. Manga Kaworu did not seem to fit the role he was suppost to be filling.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Wed Feb 10, 2010 2:55 am LiLi wrote: Personally, I don't mind the different portrayal (although I admit a re-read of the manga is long overdue on my part...) - not as a substitute to the Anime version but as a counterpoint of sorts. I would have liked to see more of AnimeKaworu. For one thing, I find him fascinating and amusing at the same time. I think giving him more screentime at least in the one episode dedicated to him, might have helped in lessening the "Kaworu is not a character, he's just a plot device" effect his appearance had on some viewers. However, I'm under the impression after looking at staff & cast interviews (Anno, Sadamoto, Ishida) and production materials that the staff didn't have a clear grasp on how to handle Kaworu's character when they made the televised ep.24. IIRC, Anno also said in the JUNE Long Interview that he had failed in his portrayal of Kaworu to an extent... I wonder whether after so many years and so many spinoffs they have a better grasp on him?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Feb 10, 2010 12:14 pm But all of the mothers in EVA are rather awful so that isn't at all unusual in that respect.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Feb 10, 2010 2:40 pm I agree completely with you AuraTwilight. Manga Kaworu seems to be little more then a insulting caricature of the original Kaworu. Created solely for the amusement of Sadamoto.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Wed Feb 10, 2010 2:44 pm My only problem with manga kaworu is he calls Shinji a bastard. I can see Kaworu killing kittens, because I always had a feeling bishonen kill kittens when no ones looking, but he always just seemed way too white bread to be using that sort of language.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Feb 10, 2010 2:47 pm Defectron wrote: he always just seemed way too white bread to be using that sort of language.
summary: []: Kaworu's Character [split from Did Not Like]             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri Feb 05, 2010 1:33 pm [Split from here .] Halicat wrote: Erm I don't really know what I don't like about the show, but I guess I would have liked it more if we got to see more of Kaworu, I felt they portrayed him as a very interesting character with a lot going on and he was a nice breath of cheery air when everything was going to shit. S
